data.narrative:
ParityQC is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Wolfgang Lechner and Magdalena Hauser. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable to this analysis. The quantum computing sector contains only 5 same-sector comparables, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single cohort—below the minimum threshold of three for reliable benchmarking. Constructing a median from the full 11-company set would obscure material differences in development stage, capital raise size, and commercial maturity between early-stage private companies and later-stage public entities.

Given the constraints of the available data, conventional triangulation methods cannot be responsibly applied. A credible valuation range for ParityQC would require either: expansion of the comparable set to capture additional Series A quantum companies with disclosed valuations, or deployment of alternative methodologies such as discounted cash flow analysis (absent revenue data here) or venture capital method analysis (requiring specific return assumptions). The fundamental uncertainty is the heterogeneity of the peer group rather than data quality. Further diligence on similar-stage fundraises within quantum hardware would be necessary to establish a supportable valuation range.
